title: Zermatt
content:
To The Matterhorn
(June-July, 1897)



    Thirty-two years since, up against the sun,
    Seven shapes, thin atomies to lower sight,
    Labouringly leapt and gained thy gabled height,
    And four lives paid for what the seven had won.

    They were the first by whom the deed was done,
    And when I look at thee, my mind takes flight
    To that day's tragic feat of manly might,
    As though, till then, of history thou hadst none.

    Yet ages ere men topped thee, late and soon
    Thou watch'dst each night the planets lift and lower;
    Thou gleam'dst to Joshua's pausing sun and moon,
    And brav'dst the tokening sky when Caesar's power
    Approached its bloody end: yea, saw'st that Noon
    When darkness filled the earth till the ninth hour.
meaning:
Thomas Hardy's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Zermatt"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
